title: Smoky Roasted Russian Cabbage Soup
description: A light, cozy Russian soup that blends charred cabbage, fermented flavor, and a subtle smokiness, all while staying Whole30-friendly with minimal ingredients.

ingredients.name: cornstarch slurry
ingredients.amount: 1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons water

instructions: Preheat the broiler to high and place a baking sheet on the middle rack.
Spread the chopped cabbage on the sheet, drizzle with a splash of olive oil, and broil for 3-4 minutes until edges are slightly charred; remove and set aside.
In a large pot, heat the remaining olive oil over medium heat and sauté the onion, carrot, celery, and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, cumin, bay leaf, salt, and pepper; stir for 1 minute to bloom the spices.
Toss in the broiled cabbage and fermented sauerkraut, then pour in the vegetable broth; bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes to let flavors meld.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook for an additional 2 minutes until the soup thickens slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, then ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh dill before serving.
